page: Microsoft Azure B32ls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity is 64.0 GB, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled, though the instance is premium storage capable, and there are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the B32ls_v2 in the average tier across most metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is 1899.38, and multi-core performance reaches 31040.35. In memory bandwidth testing, it achieves 1669.05 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 142.03 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates strong text generation performance during LLM inference with a small 135M model, reaching 292.79 tokens/sec, though performance drops to a weak 6.5 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the burstable CPU allocation model offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. However, the 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio and lack of local storage present clear tradeoffs for memory-heavy or storage-intensive applications. This server is suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that experience intermittent spikes in demand rather than sustained, continuous compute loads.
description: Microsoft Azure B32ls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bsv2 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance offers a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most categories, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 31040.35 and a static web serving throughput of 33.06 GB/sec. It exhibits strong text generation capabilities with small LLM models, though performance degrades on larger models. Given its burstable CPU allocation and lack of bundled storage, the B32ls_v2 is best suited for workloads with fluctuating resource demands, such as static web serving, software compilation, and intermittent database operations.
